reasoning: ADCs are controlled under 3A001.a.5, but for resolution >14 bits the sample rate must exceed 180 MSPS to be controlled. This 24-bit converter operates at only 256 kSPS (0.256 MSPS), far below the 3A001.a.5.5 threshold, and is not otherwise described by 3A991. Thus it is subject to the EAR but not listed on the CCL.

item_name: Quad RF-DAC / quad RF-ADC mixed-signal front-end
reasoning: The device integrates RF analog-to-digital converters operating at 4 GSPS and RF digital-to-analog converters at 12 GSPS. CCL Category 3 entry 3A001.a.5 controls high-speed data converters; the ADC channels (sample rate well above the controlled thresholds, with ~12-bit class resolution at multi-GSPS) fall under 3A001.a.5.a (analog-to-digital converters), with the DAC channels also captured under 3A001.a.5.b. The dominant controlling parameter is the high-speed ADC, so 3A001.a.5.a is the primary ECCN.

item_name: 6 GSPS RF digital-to-analog converter, to 5 GHz
reasoning: The item is a digital-to-analog converter (DAC). ECCN 3A001.a.5.b controls DACs by resolution/update rate. With 16-bit resolution (≥12 bits) and a 6 GSPS update rate exceeding the 3.5 GSPS threshold, it meets 3A001.a.5.b.2 (resolution of 12 bits or more with update rate greater than 3.5 GSPS).

item_name: Wideband 2x2 RF agile transceiver, 47 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: Integrated 2x2 RF transceiver IC with synthesizers tunable 47 MHz–6 GHz and 56 MHz channel bandwidth is a telecommunications transmission/reception component for SDR and wireless infrastructure; it does not meet the higher-performance/military thresholds of 5A001 (no frequency-hopping anti-jam, spread-spectrum, or other controlled features), so it falls under the catch-all telecommunications transmission equipment entry 5A991.b rather than Category 3 component thresholds.

A single-core 32-bit audio DSP at 300 MHz used for audio mixing/filtering/EQ is a commercial consumer/professional audio processing component. It does not meet the high-performance microprocessor/ADC/processing thresholds of Category 3 (e.g., 3A001.a.2/.a.5 or 3A991), nor is it a controlled telecom or signal-processing item under Category 4 or 5. Such a low-performance audio DSP is subject to the EAR but not described by any CCL entry, hence EAR99.

item_name: Narrow-band sub-1 GHz ISM RF transceiver
reasoning: Sub-1 GHz ISM-band FSK/GFSK RF transceiver IC is a telecommunications transmission/reception device. It is not a controlled spread-spectrum or frequency-hopping device meeting 5A001 thresholds, so it falls under the general telecommunications equipment entry 5A991 (radio/transceiver), classified under 5A991.b.4 as it is not otherwise specified.

item_name: Integrated dual-channel RF transceiver, 75 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: This is a highly integrated RF/microwave electronic component (Category 3, telecom/electronics microcircuit). Operating from 75 MHz to 6 GHz with on-chip frequency synthesizers and fast digital tuning, it most closely matches 3A001.b.7 controls on frequency synthesizer assemblies/electronic devices with rapid frequency-switching capability over wide microwave bands; the integrated wideband (200 MHz) agile transceiver with on-chip PLL/synthesizers and digital filtering exceeds general-purpose thresholds, placing it on the CCL rather than EAR99.

This is a general-purpose microprocessor/DSP system-on-chip not meeting the higher-performance military or APP thresholds of 3A001. As a commercial microprocessor/microcomputer integrated circuit, it falls under 3A991.a.1, which covers microprocessor and microcomputer microcircuits, microcontrollers, and similar devices not controlled by 3A001. The heterogeneous ARM+SHARC DSP design for audio/industrial signal processing is commercial-grade and does not meet 3A001.a.1.a adjusted-peak-performance or specialized parameters.

The IC is an information security commodity employing cryptography for data confidentiality using symmetric (AES) and asymmetric (ECDSA/ECDH) algorithms exceeding the technical thresholds in Note to 5A002.a (symmetric key >56 bits, asymmetric based on elliptic curves >112 bits). Such cryptographic controllers performing TLS/secure-boot acceleration with secure key storage are controlled under Category 5 Part 2, 5A002.a.

item_name: 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi + Bluetooth module, 4 MB flash
reasoning: This is a standard 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi (802.11 b/g/n) and Bluetooth/BLE radio-frequency transceiver module providing wireless data communication. Such commodity-grade wireless networking equipment using standard commercial frequencies and protocols is classified under Category 5 Part 1 telecommunications. It does not employ controlled cryptographic functionality beyond standard ancillary use and lacks features triggering 5A002, so it falls under 5A991 telecommunications equipment, specifically 5A991.b for transmission/reception equipment.

A general-purpose FPGA (programmable logic device) is an integrated circuit not meeting the higher performance/military thresholds of 3A001. Such commercial logic devices fall under 3A991.d, which covers integrated circuits including programmable logic arrays and field-programmable logic devices not specified elsewhere. This low-cost, low-power FPGA for glue logic/interface bridging has no special-performance characteristics warranting 3A001.

This secure element implements cryptography for data confidentiality/authentication using ECC (ECDSA/ECDH) and AES-128 symmetric algorithm with key length >56 bits, falling under Category 5 Part 2 'Information Security.' Per 5A002.a, items designed/modified to use cryptography employing symmetric algorithms with key length exceeding 56 bits or asymmetric algorithms based on discrete logarithms in elliptic curve groups exceeding 112 bits are controlled. The protected key storage, TRNG, and AES/ECC engines make this a cryptographic information security commodity controlled under 5A002.a.

This is a general-purpose 16-bit microcontroller/digital signal controller for motor control and embedded applications. Its performance (60 MIPS, modest on-chip flash/RAM, integrated DSP engine) falls well below the controlled thresholds for microprocessors/microcomputers under 3A001.a.3 and does not meet the 'adjusted peak performance' or specialized criteria of any 3A001 entry. Such commercial commodity-grade controllers are subject to the EAR but not listed on the CCL, hence EAR99.

This is standard network switching equipment. A Layer-2 managed 10/100 Ethernet switch performing routing/switching falls under telecommunications equipment in Category 5 Part 1. ECCN 5A991.b.4 covers communications switching equipment. The device performs basic Layer-2 switching at 10/100 Mbps with no controlled cryptographic functionality described, so it does not meet 5A002 thresholds. It is not a high-performance/high-speed device meeting 5A001 parameters.

A standard 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PHY transceiver compliant with IEEE 802.3 is commercial commodity networking interface hardware. It does not meet the performance thresholds or special functions controlled under Category 5 Part 1 (5A001) telecommunications equipment, nor does it perform controlled cryptographic functions under 5A002. Basic commercial Ethernet PHY ICs are not specifically described on the CCL and are classified EAR99.

A standard 10/100 Ethernet PHY transceiver operating at 100 Mbit/s with commercial networking functions does not meet the performance thresholds of telecom controls in Category 5 Part 1 (e.g. 5A001) nor any other CCL entry. Such low-speed commodity networking ICs are not specially designed for controlled applications and fall outside listed parameters, making them EAR99.

The MCU integrates a hardware cryptographic engine implementing symmetric algorithms (AES/3DES) with key lengths exceeding 56 bits and a random number generator. Under Category 5 Part 2, items designed or modified to use cryptography employing symmetric algorithms with a key length in excess of 56 bits fall under 5A002.a.1 (information security). The crypto functionality is the controlling characteristic, classifying the device on the CCL rather than as EAR99.

Short-range Bluetooth Low Energy 2.4 GHz transceiver module is a commercial wireless communications device. It does not employ controlled encryption beyond mass-market levels and lacks the spread-spectrum/frequency-hopping or military attributes of 5A001. As standard radio communications equipment not elsewhere specified, it falls under 5A991 (telecommunications), classified at 5A991.b for radio equipment using digital techniques.

item_name: Quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 compute module (2 GB / 16 GB)
reasoning: A general-purpose quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 SoM at 1.5 GHz with 2 GB RAM does not meet the high-performance thresholds of 4A003 (no APP-based supercomputing parameters) and is a consumer/commercial-grade microprocessor module. Standard interfaces (GbE, USB 2.0, HDMI, basic Wi-Fi) do not trigger Category 5 telecom or info-security controls when implementing standard published cryptography. As a mass-market low-performance computing module, it is subject to the EAR but not described by a specific CCL entry, hence EAR99.

General-purpose 32-bit ARM Cortex-M4 microcontroller (168 MHz, 1MB flash, 192KB SRAM) is a standard commercial embedded microcontroller. It does not meet the specialized parameters of 3A001 microprocessor/microcomputer controls (e.g. it is not a high-performance ADC, not radiation-hardened, and ordinary MCUs of this class are not specifically enumerated), so it is subject to the EAR but not listed on the CCL, classified EAR99.

A standard 1-inch broadband dielectric mirror for the 400-750 nm visible band on fused-silica does not meet 6A004 optics thresholds (no specialized scan, deformable, or high damage-threshold coatings >1064 nm/specified parameters) and is not otherwise described on the CCL, so it falls under EAR99.

A simple mechanical kinematic mirror mount for 1-inch optics is a passive optomechanical positioning fixture with no optical performance characteristics of its own. It does not meet the specialized control parameters of Category 6 (lasers, sensors, optics) such as mirror flatness/coating specs in 6A004, nor any Category 2 precision positioning thresholds. As a general-purpose lab opto-mechanical component subject to the EAR but not described by any CCL entry, it is classified EAR99.

A general-purpose single-core 32-bit ARM Cortex-A8 applications microprocessor at 1.0 GHz does not meet the performance thresholds or specialized characteristics of controlled microprocessors in Category 3 (e.g., 3A001.a.1 covers integrated circuits with specific radiation-hardening, temperature, or other military/specialized features). Standard commercial embedded/HMI microprocessors of this type are not described by any CCL entry and are classified EAR99.
